description: Powers drawing on the living world — commanding plants, bonding with animals, shapeshifting into wild forms, and thriving in the wilderness.
abilities: Animal Form

Taking the shape of a wild animal no smaller than a mouse and no larger than a horse, adopting its form, senses, and range of motion. Everything carried melds into the new shape. The animal form has moderate vitality and can make basic natural attacks. Spellcasting, item use, and spoken language are impossible while transformed. The caster may revert at any time, regaining the vitality they had before shifting. Losing all vitality in animal form forces an instant return to the original shape.
Animal Partner

Recruiting a nearby animal companion, no smaller than a mouse and no larger than a horse, who follows the caster's orders to the best of its ability. Only one partner may be kept at a time. The animal acts alongside the caster in dangerous situations, refuses to willingly leave, and will search for the caster if separated. Though it cannot speak, it vaguely senses its partner's mood and intentions. The companion has moderate vitality and can make basic attacks.
Command Nature

Entreating nearby plants with a whispered rhyming couplet, coaxing trees to rustle, leaves to fall, flowers to bloom, vines to expand, and seeds to sprout. The poem must be one the plants might appreciate, spoken aloud with care. At greater power, the caster can weaponize the growth -- thorned vines lashing a creature or a tree shaking its acorns down violently -- inflicting minor damage on a nearby target.
Courier
Finding a friendly animal nearby and whispering instructions for it to deliver a small, light item to a specific place or person within a day's travel.
Evening Star

Summoning a wisp of light high into the open sky directly above the caster. For the next hour the wisp sheds daylight across a huge area, illuminating the distant landscape. It follows the caster's movement, always hovering directly overhead.
Friend of the Land

Claiming kinship with a wilderness region of personal meaning -- a forest, a valley, a stretch of coast -- that is not already dominated by a powerful enemy. Every living thing in the region recognizes the caster as its ally. Tracking, navigating, communing with trees, and blending into the land all become effortless within the chosen territory. Upon becoming friend of the land, a group of volunteer creatures construct a natural fort at a chosen location over the course of a week -- something in the spirit of a giant anthill, a meerkat manor, a nest among the treetops, or a beaver's dam. The fort holds up to twenty rooms, including a kitchen, a great hall, and an armory. Animal sentries watch the keep around the clock and alert the caster to intruders or guests, while a small staff of volunteers routinely forages to keep it stocked with fruits, vegetables, and other provisions. They occasionally invite the caster to play.
Gills

Enchanting a small container of water so that when spritzed on a creature, small but visible gills sprout along the neck, granting the ability to breathe underwater for up to an hour. A single enchantment produces enough water for several applications.
Memories of Stone

Touching a stone monument -- a mountain face, a canyon wall, a weathered statue -- and sharing with it a personal story about family or memory. The monument reciprocates with ancient wisdom, revealing whether this world is real and natural or some kind of machination, whether long-lost monuments lie buried beneath the surface such as hidden temples or ancient vaults, and a vague sense of the most powerful lost artifact in the world and the region where it might rest.
Navigate

Feeling the land in one's bones, making it impossible to become lost in wilderness unless magic interferes. At greater power, the caster guides companions to a specific type of discovery while traveling: an oasis offering a small amount of nourishment, a natural cave providing shelter from harsh weather, minor ruins such as an abandoned keep or a weathered statue, or the home of an animal or group of animals like an otter's den.
Pair Bond

Developing a deep telepathic bond with an animal partner that persists until a new one is recruited. The caster passively experiences the animal's senses -- catching glimpses of what it sees, hears, and feels, along with a shadow of pain when it is harmed. At any time, the caster may enter a trance to leave their own body and take direct control of the partner, retaining dim awareness of their own surroundings. Speech is impossible through the animal, but the trance may be exited at will.
Shapeshift

A mastered form of animal transformation, allowing the caster to assume the shape of any creature from as small as a housefly to as large as an elephant. The animal form is more resilient and strikes harder than a basic transformation. The caster may communicate telepathically with allies while shifted.
Shift Season

Manipulating the elements to change the current season across a vast area centered on the caster's location. Any season native to the world may be chosen, and the effect lasts at least through the following day. The spell takes a minute to cast, with the transformation radiating rapidly outward. Results range from masterful control -- choosing both the season and exact weather for an extended duration -- to unpredictable outcomes where the season shifts but a fierce storm rolls in, or at worst a natural disaster forms unbidden.
Shroud

Entreating nearby flora to shelter the party while camping in the wilderness. Shrubs and thorny vines emerge around the campsite, concealing even the light of a small campfire. The shroud persists until the party breaks camp. If hostile creatures approach, the trees rustle and howl to provide early warning. Creatures can force their way through the barrier, but the thorns draw blood as they pass. Resting within the shroud grants the full benefits of a peaceful rest.
Signal

Creating a distress signal visible to other rangers while in the wilderness -- a column of smoke, a message carved into bark, or a similar sign. Within the next day, a fellow ranger arrives to offer aid. Out of professional respect, they stay until they have finished helping with a single request, though they are an independent soul who acts according to their own interests and ideals. They arrive well-equipped and capable in a fight.
Speak with Animal

Touching a non-hostile animal to form a brief telepathic bond, gaining the ability to interpret its thoughts and feelings. The animal can share basic information -- how it feels, what it has seen, what it wants -- but complex ideas are beyond its understanding.
Speak with Trees

Touching a tree trunk to connect with the trees of a contiguous forest. Through this bond the caster may ask the trees to search the forest -- locating a specific creature, an object lying somewhere within, or a place like a cave or building -- and the trees commune among themselves before sharing the location, the route, and how long it will take. Alternatively, the caster may ask the trees to stand watch over the party; until the group leaves the forest, trees sway and rustle with increasing urgency as traps or dangerous creatures draw near.
Thorn

Opening the palm to conjure a poisonous thorn and shoot it in a straight line toward a nearby target, dealing minor damage. When the shot lands perfectly on a living creature, the poison swells the target's skin, blinding them and sealing their voice and magic for a brief moment.
Whisper on the Wind

Whispering a message that rides the wind in all directions, requiring a clear line of sight to the sky. The message summons a flying creature -- a raven, a griffin, or any winged beast -- which arrives within the hour. So long as the caster treats it kindly, the creature obeys commands for the next day: delivering messages, giving rides, causing distractions. It will not attack enemies unless provoked first.
Wild Celebrity

Commanding such respect among wild creatures that none will attack the caster unless harmed first, though trained animals and pets may still be commanded against them. The caster's ability to speak with animals and trees becomes effortless, and any animal partner grows stronger -- more resilient and capable of striking multiple times. At the height of this power, the caster sends a call through the wilderness to recruit a small army of native animals that stay for the next day, acting together as a unified force capable of distributing their attacks across several targets.
Wild Evolution

Permanently intertwining the caster's physical essence with that of a chosen animal form, no smaller than a mouse and no larger than an elephant, selected once and forever. The caster may shift freely between their original form and the animal at will, retaining their current vitality across transformations. In animal form, the caster adopts the creature's natural capabilities -- flight as an eagle, swimming as a dolphin -- and strikes with powerful natural attacks. Spells may be cast and allies spoken to telepathically while transformed.
Wild Font

Touching a container holding food, water, or oil and enchanting it for a brief duration so that its contents surge forth in generous surplus, spilling and overflowing -- a pitcher of water flooding the floor, a sack of grain bursting open into a large pile.
World Wish

Becoming the seed for a new world -- a spell that consumes the caster's life forever, with no possibility of return. The caster's entire knowledge of nature manifests as their body dissipates into radiant light, releasing a cascading wave of energy that transforms the planet. The wave first alters the atmosphere to support life, then a blooming cascade of flora emanates outward, overtaking the entire surface. Oceans and rivers form where none existed. If the caster wills it, all constructed objects -- buildings, infrastructure -- are gracefully disintegrated, returning the land to nature while gently lowering living things to the ground unharmed. This permanent transformation cannot be stopped, interrupted, or reversed.
